text: Lɛmɛ Yesu tábinya onukpɛ̃. Ade Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a buki ebi ye kĩ, “Ntani Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a kĩ, bi boe ntɛ nɔ nyɛ Kristo, Baguma Obi a.”
translated: Jesus' name is powerful. The Holy Spirit told Philip, "The Baguma people are waiting for you, because they want to know Christ, the Baguma people."

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Midã kɔvɔlabi, kɔádu funitsã, kɔákpɔta funitsã kako nɛ tili kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a adɔɔla koe! Mbɔɛɛ mitátɛ kɔya miba kɔvɔlabi?
translated: The owner of the farm, the owner of the goats, the owner of the sheep all came to me, and I, Olegha, have settled the matter for them! Why should I still be called the farm owner?

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, ɔkɔlɔ a naa mɔ lugo flee ade mpo akũ ɔwɛ kabɔa ye kato katɛ̃mɛna, kitonɛkĩ ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kavũ katsɛlamɔ ye.
translated: When he went to the farm, the man left his child at home and told him not to let anyone enter the house.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mila ndɛ ɔhaĩ mikpa batidziwa flee. Bɛláapuli bɛkɔla ɔmatɔ kĩ bitsua nɛ ɔtɔ akũ.
translated: The child went to the farm to harvest cassava. The cassava leaves were cooked and eaten with palm oil.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Libe wo be flee. Lɛmɛ lɛtalɛta Yesu lɛmɔ be kɔdzɛla kĩ, “Mitavila! Yĩ manɛ. Mina ɔwɔlɛ!”
translated: The people were afraid. Jesus' disciple Peter said to him, "Master, it's me. Don't be afraid!"

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Onyole kĩĩ dzɛ̃ mɔ lugo fɔmɔ ɛmɔ Yesu. Foesũ ewula atsɛ ɛnake aduli nɛ Yesu nnyɛ.
translated: The people said that he was going to the house of Jesus. But they did not find him because Jesus had disappeared.

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Foesũ mifɔnɛ be awɛ. Bɛnyɛ banɛkunɛ kĩ bakpã banɛkunɛ, ade ntɛ ɔnɛkunɛ akpã ɔnɛkunɛ ni, be nviã a flee baatɔ nɛ lɛkɛ kamɛ.”
translated: The children went to the farm. They saw a squirrel and a monkey, and then they saw a snake, and the child ran back home crying.

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Kĩ Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a katsua ye onukpɛ̃ malo ni, Yesu tálɛ kadima.
translated: When the people of Judah were in exile and suffering, Jesus came to save them.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Yohanes do tɛtadiɛ kĩ bɛkɔa ɔpɔnkɔpoku tɛtsã bɛnɔ, ade ɛkɔa ɔyaɛ ovoli abɔsɔ eboomu kate a. Ye funitsã nyɛ koolo mɔ kɔsã.
translated: Yohanes went to the farm and found a dead horse lying there, and he carried it home and buried it behind the house. He did not tell anyone about it.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Kitonɛkĩ kayɔkanyɛ futefute deĩ kĩ foe sũ banyole lɛláapuli bɛkɔã batsɔ̃. Badi bɛma be basaɛ, babɛbã bati lɛkɛna be basaɛ, ade badi lɛmɛ lákɔã batsɔ̃ nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a sũ. Oti a kĩ yaapuli ɛtɛ fɔtsa katsa kĩĩ ni, ɛtɛ foe.” Yesu Hila Kubisɛ̃ɛ̃.
translated: The people were bringing little children to him so that he might touch them, but the disciples rebuked them. When Jesus saw this, he was indignant and said to them, “Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these. Truly I tell you, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.” And he took the children in his arms, placed his hands on them and blessed them. Jesus Christ is Lord.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Kutse wĩ awũ babi wĩ, ade kutse kpa lɛmɛ awũ babi kpa a.
translated: The child who is not his father's, the child does not resemble the father.

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, ɔkɔlɔ a naa mɔ lugo flee ade mpo akũ ɔwɛ kabɔa ye kato katɛ̃mɛna, kitonɛkĩ ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kavũ katsɛlamɔ ye.
translated: When the sun rose, the man went to the farm and found that a leopard had killed a goat, so he took the leopard and tied it to a tree.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: Nɛ foe kawũna kamɛ a, edo ye obi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃, ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Matɛ kado kĩ baanyɔã yĩ obi.’
translated: When he saw the child, he said to his wife, ‘Let us go and leave the child here.’

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Lɔkɔa nde sũ miaha akũ nɛ tilima mɔ tɛtadiɛ akũ? Midã ayekĩ lupo kamɛ futse ako nhuilɛ kabo anɛ, fɔákɛna adzuma, fɔátaɛ tileti kanɔ tilima kakpa akũ.
translated: Why is it that when we go to the farm we do not take the old hoe? I think the reason is that it is blunt and cannot dig well, so we leave it at home, and we take the new hoe to the farm.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: Nɔ mɔawɛ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa baakɔa bɛdzɛmɔ nɔ bɛkpa nɔ ɔtɔbɛ ĩye bɛkɔ nɔ kɔtɔ.” Mbla Batsanɛ Mɔ Farisifɔ Via Awãwã Fɔtsa Bitomɛna Yesu Sɛkɛ̃.
translated: “When the Pharisees saw that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together. Then one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.” This is how the Pharisees came to Jesus.

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Kĩ Yesu do ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a fɔtɔ eyua a, ɛdzakũ nɛ awã ɛnaa tɛmatɔ a kĩ tifuã a kamɛ kĩ yanaatsa fɔtsa, ede lɛtsã nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a lɛmɛ akũ.
translated: When Jesus had finished giving instructions to his twelve disciples, he went away from there to teach and preach in their towns.

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Kĩ asau a yi mɔ kɔfɔabi a, bɛvɛlɛ ye bɛyamɛna nɛ mpo abuĩ, bidzi bɛsa kɔfɔabi a kamɛ. Bɛkɔa kɔfɔabi wĩ a biko nɛ fɔwɔda kamɛ ade bɛtalɛ kɔkpa a biwula a.
translated: When they reached the place, they found that the people had already left, so they returned home. They said that the people had gone to the farm and would return in the evening.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, baanyɛ mi bɛkpa kĩ bɛkɛna mi awɔda, bɛkɔ mi malo. Batidziwa flee aakesĩ mi nɛ yĩ sũ.
translated: When I was coming, they told me that they had beaten my brother and tied him up, and that they had taken my money. The police did not arrest anyone for me.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Foesũ ebi be kĩ, ‘Mi malo minaakɛna adzuma nɛ yĩ waĩ kɔba kamɛ, maase mi kɔtɔ kĩ kɛfɛta a.’
translated: The child said, 'I will not go to farm today because I am sick, let me stay and rest.'

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Lɛtsa a kĩ Yesu kɛna a, kɛna bati kɔdabu a flee a saa, ade bivia kĩ, “Mbɔɛɛ nnɛ́ Dawid Obi a nɛ awĩ a?”
translated: When Jesus passed by, some blind men followed him, saying, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: “Ye ɔlate kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Nɔ ɔsumunɛ kpa mɔ ɔnyɔnɛpo! Ntɛ onyina kĩ mana fɔtsa nɛ awã kĩ ntádu foe, ade makpɔta fɔtsa nɛ awã kĩ ntásã foe a ni,
translated: He said to his wife, ‘I have found a place to sleep! But you must not go there with your left foot, and you must not go there with your right foot either,
